cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI introduced its new GPT-5.6 model family with improvements across cybersecurity and other areas. The models will continue powering Microsoft's Copilot 365 suite.

cards.title: Anthropic Launches Rupee Pricing for India
cards.body: Claude users in India can now access Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans. India has become Anthropic's biggest market after the US.

cards.title: Meta Removes Controversial Instagram AI Feature
cards.body: Meta discontinued an AI feature that referenced public Instagram content after user backlash. The company acknowledged the feature 'missed the mark' in its implementation.

cards.title: Google Integrates Gemini into Waze
cards.body: Waze is adding new AI-powered features powered by Google's Gemini assistant. The integration reflects Google's broader push to embed Gemini across products.

cards.title: OpenAI Shutters AI Browser Atlas
cards.body: OpenAI is discontinuing its Atlas AI browser after less than a year. Agentic browsing features are moving to the desktop app and Chrome extension.

cards.title: ChatGPT Expands Focus on Family Users
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager for family and household experiences. The role will focus on caregivers and older adults using ChatGPT.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple filed a lawsuit alleging OpenAI misappropriated trade secrets with direction from senior leadership. A longtime Apple former employee is also implicated in the allegations.

cards.title: SK Hynix Achieves Record US IPO
cards.body: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the largest foreign IPO in US history. The AI chip boom milestone comes amid calls for new US manufacturing facilities.

cards.title: Lyzr AI Agent Raises $100M Using AI
cards.body: Lyzr used its own AI agent to manage its $100 million fundraising round. The successful deployment demonstrates practical enterprise AI agent capabilities.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark 1.1 Coding AI
cards.body: Meta entered AI coding with Muse Spark 1.1, designed for large agentic workloads and bug fixes. The tool targets enterprises seeking code migration automation.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo departed from her Chief Operating Officer role after extended medical leave. Her exit creates leadership challenges as OpenAI pursues an IPO.

cards.title: Hugging Face: Open Source AI Booming
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue reports open source AI momentum, with the platform used by roughly half of Fortune 500. Companies are increasingly moving away from renting proprietary models.

cards.title: New York Times: OpenAI Hid Evidence
cards.body: Publishers allege OpenAI concealed tools and datasets that identify copyrighted content in ChatGPT outputs. The motion for sanctions escalates the ongoing copyright lawsuit.

cards.title: Google Requires AI Disclosure in Ads
cards.body: Google expanded AI disclosure requirements beyond election ads to all synthetically created content. Advertisers must now reveal when AI generated or altered ad creative.

cards.title: Musk Praises Anthropic's AI Ambitions
cards.body: Elon Musk endorsed Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models while pledging not to 'cut off' the company. The commitment addresses concerns about Anthropic hosting models on his infrastructure.

cards.title: Altman Fires Back at Musk Over AI
cards.body: Sam Altman responded to Musk's scammer accusations by criticizing OpenAI's rival's space datacenter strategy. Altman claimed Musk was misleading public investors on short-term infrastructure promises.

cards.title: AI ROI Question Reaches $3 Trillion
cards.body: The AI return-on-investment debate has intensified with significantly larger financial stakes. The discussion carries major consequences for enterprise AI adoption strategies.

cards.title: Exploring Alignment in User-Centered AI
cards.body: The industry debates what total user-aligned AI actually means in practice. Ethical implications extend to controversial scenarios across different application domains.

cards.title: Sam Altman Responds to Musk's Space Datacenter Criticism
cards.body: Altman fired back at Musk's accusations of being a scammer, claiming Musk is the one selling public market investors on short-term space datacenters. The exchange highlights ongoing tensions between the two tech leaders.

cards.title: Anthropic Localizes Claude Pricing for India Market
cards.body: Claude users in India can now subscribe using Indian rupee-denominated plans. This marks Anthropic's effort to expand beyond the US, its largest market.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Powers New Waze AI Features
cards.body: Waze is integrating Google's Gemini AI assistant into its navigation app with new AI-powered features and customization options. The move reflects Google's broader strategy to embed Gemini across its product suite.

cards.title: ChatGPT Hires Product Manager for Family Features
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The expansion signals OpenAI's push to deepen household adoption.

cards.title: Meta Removes Controversial AI Feature on Instagram
cards.body: Meta removed an AI feature that generated content based on users' public posts after significant backlash. The company acknowledged the feature missed the mark with users.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secret Theft
cards.body: Apple alleges OpenAI misappropriated trade secrets, with misconduct allegedly directed by senior leadership including a former employee. The lawsuit marks another legal challenge for OpenAI.

cards.title: Hugging Face CEO: Open Source AI Is Booming
cards.body: Hugging Face has grown into a GitHub-like platform for AI where developers share models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. CEO Clem Delangue says open source AI matters more than ever.

cards.title: SK Hynix's Record IPO Fuels AI Chip Boom
cards.body: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, marking a major Wall Street moment for the AI chip sector. The company is now being urged to build new US manufacturing facilities.

cards.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Becomes Preferred Microsoft Copilot Model
cards.body: OpenAI's new family of models will continue powering Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps. The partnership underscores continued integration despite breakup speculation.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Fidji Simo Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo is departing her full-time role as OpenAI's No. 2 after extended medical leave. The departure creates a leadership gap amid the company's IPO plans and competition with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI's latest model family promises improvements across multiple areas including cybersecurity capabilities. The release represents the company's continued evolution of its core AI technology.

cards.title: AI Agent Startup Lyzr Raises $100M Using Its Own Agent
cards.body: Lyzr used its own AI agent to autonomously conduct a $100 million fundraising round. The feat demonstrates the practical viability of agentic AI systems for complex business tasks.

cards.title: OpenAI Shutters Atlas Browser After One Year
cards.body: OpenAI is discontinuing its AI-powered Atlas browser but moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and Chrome extension. The pivot suggests a shift in strategy for AI-powered web interaction.

cards.title: Musk Vows Not to Cut Off Anthropic's Access
cards.body: Elon Musk praised Anthropic's Mythos/Fable and promised not to restrict the company's access to his hosting infrastructure. With $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk is reassuring Anthropic about partnership stability.

cards.title: Meta Enters AI Coding Market with Muse Spark 1.1
cards.body: Meta's AI coding tool Spark handles large agentic workloads, fixes bugs, and automates code migrations for enterprises. The release intensifies competition in the rapidly growing AI coding assistant space.

cards.title: New York Times Accuses OpenAI of Hidden Evidence
cards.body: The New York Times alleges OpenAI concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. Publishers are escalating their copyright lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

cards.title: Google Requires AI Disclosure in Ads
cards.body: Google will now mandate disclosure of AI-generated or digitally altered content in advertisements beyond just election ads. The policy addresses growing concerns about synthetic media and consumer transparency.

cards.title: Anthropic Unveils Hidden Space in Claude's Thinking
cards.body: Anthropic developed a technique revealing how Claude's internal representations work when solving problems, finding both mundane and unsettling patterns. The research offers the clearest view yet into large language model reasoning.

cards.title: Anthropic's Latest Discovery About AI Reasoning
cards.body: Anthropic, valued at nearly $1 trillion, published research uncovering strange behaviors in how AI models think through concepts. The findings add to the company's reputation for publishing innovative AI research.

cards.title: AI Defenders Embrace Prompt Injection Attacks
cards.body: Security researchers are exploring how defenders can leverage prompt injection techniques to protect AI systems. The approach represents a counterintuitive strategy in AI security.

cards.title: World Models: Promise and Limitations
cards.body: AI world models offer potential for simulating complex environments but face significant practical limits. The technology represents an emerging frontier in AI research and development.

cards.title: Quantum Error Correction Enables Processor Recalibration
cards.body: Quantum processors can now continuously recalibrate themselves using advanced error correction techniques. The breakthrough addresses a critical challenge in making quantum computing more practical.
